figures.caption: Table 1: Comparison of 7-scenes and NRGBD. Lower is better for Acc/Comp, higher is better for NC. We follow Point3R [38] to use “GA” to mark methods with global alignment, and use “Optim.” and “Onl.” to distinguish between optimization-based and online methods.
Figure 4: Visualized results of reconstruction on datasets NRGBD and 7scenes.

Table 3: Camera Pose Estimation Evaluation. Best and second-best results are highlighted separately for optimization-based and online methods. Since global optimization-based methods generally have an advantage over online methods in camera pose estimation, we compare the performance of global optimization approaches and online approaches separately.
Table 2: Depth evaluation on NYU-v2 (Static), Sintel, Bonn, and KITTI. Lower is better for Abs Rel; higher is better for 𝛿 < 1.25.

Table 4: Ablation study on different memory cache update strategies. Best results are highlighted in bold and second-best results are underlined. Our retain-or-replace strategy is denoted as ‘random’ in the table.

Figure 2: Overview of the proposed ray-aware pointer-based streaming reconstruction pipeline. Each incoming frame is encoded into dense image features and interacts with a persistent 3D pointer memory through a pointer–image interaction module. Dedicated heads predict camera pose, depth, and point representations in a unified global coordinate system. Each pointer stores explicit spatial elements, including 3D position, viewing direction, frame timestamp, and learned features. The Memory Encoder module and Adaptive Points Update module incrementally refine the pointer set using pose-aware geometric consistency, selectively preserving informative pointers while filtering redundant observations, enabling stable and continuous streaming 3D reconstruction.

Figure 3: Illustration of the pointer update results for a given frame using the merge strategy and our retain_or_replacemethod. Blue pointers are replaced by red pointers. The metrics shown below the image represent the quantitative reconstruction performance of the current scene and the number of pointers stored in the memory cache after reconstruction. Our method significantly reduces the number of pointers stored in thememory cache while achieving improved reconstruction performance.
